item.title: Evaluating the impact of therapeutic drug monitoring strategies on clinical outcomes in paediatric inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - The increasing incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) in paediatric populations underscores the importance of effective management strategies. Anti-tumor necrosis factor (anti-TNF) agents, s...

item.title: Impact of 5-ASA discontinuation in children with ulcerative colitis on biologic therapy: A propensity score-matched study.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - 5-Aminosalicylic acid (5-ASA) is recommended as a first-line medication in mild to moderate ulcerative colitis (UC), but indications regarding its use in children with moderate to severe disease tr...

item.title: High-Dose Opioid Use Among Adolescents and Young Adults with Chronic Digestive Disorders.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - High-dose opioid use (≥ 50 morphine milligram equivalents [MME]/day) is associated with increased risk of adverse outcomes, including overdose. We investigated the prevalence and risk factors for h...

item.title: Upper and small bowel Crohn's disease in Brazilian children: Phenotypic characteristic and surgical risk.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - Upper and small bowel Crohn's disease (U-SBCD) represents a clinically aggressive phenotype with high complication rates yet remains diagnostically challenging. In low- and middle-income countries ...

item.title: Multi-system inflammatory syndrome in a child with Crohn's disease- a diagnostic dilemma.
item.description: Oxford medical case reports - Case report - Post-covid multisystem inflammatory syndrome in children (MIS-C) can cause life-threatening inflammation in children, particularly in those with underlying conditions like Crohn's disease (CD). The...

item.title: Five children with haploinsufficiency of A20 caused by heterozygous mutations in the TNFAIP3 gene.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Case report - Children with A20 haploinsufficiency, resulting from heterozygous variants in the TNFAIP3 gene, are increasingly being identified. However, their diagnosis and treatment remain challenging and are ...

item.title: Efficacy and safety of dual-targeted therapy in children with inflammatory bowel disease: Retrospective cohort study.
item.description: World journal of clinical pediatrics - There is a limited number of studies on the efficacy and safety of dual-targeted therapy (DTT) in children with inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD); the latest reports focus on the combination of bio...

item.title: Gastrointestinal consequences of cesarean section birth: A systematic review of short- and long-term effects in infancy and beyond.
item.description: World journal of clinical pediatrics - Cesarean section (C-section) delivery rates are rising globally, raising concern about their potential long-term health consequences on offspring. Emerging evidence suggests that C-section, by bypa...
